member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress
member.summary: Represents an IrDA device address.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress.#ctor(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified address.
member.param: Address as 4 byte array.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified address.
member.param: representation of the address.

members.member:
Returns the IrDA address as an integer.
- An .

Returns the internal byte array.
- An array of .

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress.TryParse(System.String,InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress@)
member.summary: Converts the string representation of an address to it's equivalent. A return value indicates whether the operation succeeded.
member.param: A string containing an address to convert.

When this method returns, contains the equivalent to the address contained in s, if the conversion succeeded, or null (Nothing in Visual Basic) if the conversion failed. The conversion fails if the s parameter is null or is not of the correct format.
member.returns: true if s is a valid IrDA address; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress.Parse(System.String)
member.summary: Converts the string representation of an IrDA address to a new instance.
member.param: A string containing an address to convert.
member.remarks: Address must be specified in hex format optionally separated by the colon or period character e.g. 00000000, 00:00:00:00 or 00.00.00.00.
member.exception: irdaString is not a valid IrDA address.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress.ToString
member.summary: Converts the address to its equivalent string representation.
member.returns: The string representation of this instance.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress.ToString(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a representation of the value of this instance, according to the provided format specifier.
member.param: A single format specifier that indicates how to format the value of this Guid. The format parameter can be "N", "C" or "P". If format is null or the empty string (""), "N" is used.
member.returns: A representation of the value of this .

members.member:
Compares two instances for equality.
- The to compare with the current instance.
- true if is a and equal to the current instance; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns the hash code for this instance.
member.returns: A hash code for the current object.

members.member:
Returns an indication whether the values of two specified objects are equal.
- A or . A or . - true if the values of the two instance are equal; otherwise, false.

Returns an indication whether the values of two specified objects are not equal.
- A or . A or . - true if the value of the two instance is different; otherwise, false.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress.None
member.summary: Provides a null IrDA address.

members.member:
Returns a representation of the value of this instance, according to the provided format specifier.
A single format specifier that indicates how to format the value of this Guid. The format parameter can be "N", "C" or "P". If format is null or the empty string (""), "N" is used. Ignored. - A representation of the value of this . - See for the possible format strings and their output.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.IrDA.IrDAAttributeType
member.summary: Defines the type of an IAS attribute.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.IrDA.IrDAAttributeType.Integer
member.summary: Identifies an integer attribute value.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.IrDA.IrDAAttributeType.OctetSequence
member.summary: Identifies a binary, or octet, attribute value.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.IrDA.IrDAAttributeType.String
member.summary: Identifies a string attribute value.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.IrDA.IrDAService
member.summary: Standard IrDA service names.

member.summary: Well-known Service Name “IrDA:IrCOMM”

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.IrDA.IrDAService.IrLpt
member.summary: Well-known Service Name “IrLPT”

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.IrDA.IrDAService.ObjectExchange

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet
member.summary: Describes the character sets supported by the device.
member.remarks: The enumeration describes the following character sets, which are used by the and classes.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ASCII
member.summary: The ASCII character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ISO8859Latin1
member.summary: The western European graphic character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ISO8859Latin2
member.summary: The eastern European graphic character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ISO8859Latin3
member.summary: The southern European graphic character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ISO8859Latin4
member.summary: The northern European graphic character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ISO8859Cyrillic
member.summary: The Cyrillic graphic character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ISO8859Arabic
member.summary: The Arabic graphic character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ISO8859Greek
member.summary: The Greek graphic character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ISO8859Hebrew
member.summary: The Hebrew graphic character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.ISO8859Latin5
member.summary: The Turkish graphic character set.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet.Unicode
member.summary: The Unicode character set.

members.member:
Makes connections to services on peer IrDA devices.
-
Makes connections to services on peer IrDA devices. It allows discovery of all devices in range, then a connection can be made to the required service on the chosen peer. Or, given just the service name a connection will be made to an arbitrary peer. This is most useful when it is expected that there will be only one device in range—as is often the case.
It can be used with both the full and Compact frameworks, and can be used as a replacement for the latter's built-in version simply by changing the referenced namespace and assembly. It also has features extra to those in the CF's version. For instance, following the pattern of in framework version 2, it provides access to the underlying via a Client property. This is particularly useful as it allows setting socket options, for instance IrCOMM Cooked mode with option .
There a number of well-known services, a few are listed here.
Service description Service Name, Protocol type
OBEX file transfer OBEX:IrXfer, (TinyTP)
OBEX general OBEX, (TinyTP)
Printing IrLPT, IrLMP mode
IrCOMM e.g. to modems IrDA:IrCOMM, IrCOMM 9-Wire/Cooked mode
The modes required by the last two are set by socket option, as noted for IrCOMM above.
Of course the library also includes specific OBEX protocol support, both client and server, see etc.
- Example code to connect to a IrCOMM service would be as follows.
Public Shared Sub Main() Dim cli As New IrDAClient ' Set IrCOMM Cooked/9-wire mode. cli.Client.SetSocketOption(IrDASocketOptionLevel.IrLmp, _ IrDASocketOptionName.NineWireMode, _ 1) ' equivalent to 'True' ' Connect cli.Connect("IrDA:IrCOMM") ' Connected, now send and receive e.g. by using the ' NetworkStream returned by cli.GetStream ... End Sub
-

Initializes a new instance of the class, and optionally connects to a peer device.
----
Initializes a new instance of the class.
It then allows discovery of all devices in range using , then a connection can be made to the required service on the chosen peer using . Or, given just the service name a connection will be made to an arbitrary peer, using . This is most useful when it is expected that there will be only one device in range — as is often the case.

Initializes a new instance of the class and connects to the specified service name.
-
This is equivalent to calling the default constructor followed by .
As noted the connection will be made to an arbitrary peer. This is most useful when it is expected that there will be only one device in range — as is often the case. If a connection is to be made to a particular remote peer, then use the
overload.
Infrared connections are made by specifying a Service Name, which can be any value provided the participating devices refer the same name.
See for the errors that can occur.
-
A containing the service name to connect to.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAClient.#ctor(InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class and connects to the specified endpoint.

remarks.para: This is equivalent to calling the default constructor followed by .
The endpoint specifies both the peer device and service name to connect to. If only one device is expected to be in range, or an arbitrary peer device is suitable, then one can use instead.

member.param: An initialised with the address of the peer device and the service name to connect to.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAClient.Active
member.summary: Gets or set a value that indicates whether a connection has been made.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAClient.Available
member.summary: The number of bytes of data received from the network and available to be read.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAClient.Client
member.summary: Gets or sets the underlying .
member.remarks: This is particularly useful as it allows setting socket options, for instance IrCOMM Cooked mode, ie .
member.example:
Example code to connect to a IrCOMM service would be as follows, note the use of the Client property.
Public Shared Sub Main() Dim cli As New IrDAClient ' Set IrCOMM Cooked/9-wire mode. cli.Client.SetSocketOption( _ IrDASocketOptionLevel.IrLmp, _ IrDASocketOptionName.NineWireMode, _ 1) ' representing true ' Connect cli.Connect("IrDA:IrCOMM") ' Connected, now send and receive e.g. by using the ' NetworkStream returned by cli.GetStream ... End Sub

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAClient.Connected
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the underlying for an is connected to a remote host.

members.member:
Obtains information about available devices.
-
Returns a maximum of 8 devices, for more flexibility use the other overloads.
- The discovered devices as an array of .

Obtains information about a specified number of devices.
- The maximum number of devices to get information about. - The discovered devices as an array of .

Obtains information about available devices using a socket.
- The maximum number of devices to get information about. A to be uses to run the discovery process. It should have been created for the IrDA protocol - The discovered devices as an array of .

Gets the name of the peer device using the specified socket.
A connected IrDA Socket. The name of the remote device. -
This finds the name of the device to which the socket is connection, an exception will occur if the socket is not connected.
-
s is null (Nothing in Visual Basic).
The remote device is not present in the list of discovered devices.
The socket is not connected.

Gets the name of the peer device participating in the communication.
-
This finds the name of the device to which the client is connection, an exception will occur if the socket is not connected.
-
If the remote device is not found in the discovery cache.
The socket is not connected.

Forms a connection to the specified peer service.
--
Forms a connection to the specified endpoint.
The endpoint specifies both the peer device and service name to connect to. If only one device is expected to be in range, or an arbitrary peer device is suitable, then one can use instead.
An initialised with the address of the peer device and the service name to connect to.

Forms a connection to the specified service on an arbitrary peer.
As noted the connection will be made to an arbitrary peer. This is most useful when it is expected that there will be only one device in range — as is often the case. If a connection is to be made to a particular remote peer, then use .
The Service Name to connect to eg "OBEX". In the very uncommon case where a connection is to be made to a specific LSAP-SEL (port number), then use the form "LSAP-SELn", where n is an integer. -
No peer IrDA device was found. The exception has message “No device”.
A connection could not be formed. See the exception message or
for what error occurred.

Begins an asynchronous request for a remote host connection.
-
Begins an asynchronous request for a remote host connection. The remote host is specified by an endpoint.
-
An initialised with the address of the peer device and the service name to connect to.
An AsyncCallback delegate that references the method to invoke when the operation is complete. A user-defined object that contains information about the connect operation. This object is passed to the requestCallback delegate when the operation is complete. - An that represents the asynchronous connect, which could still be pending.

Begins an asynchronous request for a remote host connection. The remote host is specified by a service name (string).
- The service name of the remote host. An AsyncCallback delegate that references the method to invoke when the operation is complete. A user-defined object that contains information about the connect operation. This object is passed to the requestCallback delegate when the operation is complete. - An that represents the asynchronous connect, which could still be pending.
-
See for the errors that can occur.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAClient.EndConnect(System.IAsyncResult)
member.summary: Asynchronously accepts an incoming connection attempt.
member.param: An object returned by a call to / .

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAClient.ConnectAsync(InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint,System.Object)
member.summary: Connects the client to the specified service on an arbitrary peer as an asynchronous operation.
member.param: An initialised with the address of the peer device and the service name to connect to.

members.member:
Closes the and the underlying connection.
- The two XxxxxClient classes produced by Microsoft (TcpClient, and IrDAClient in the NETCF) have various documented behaviours and various actual behaviours for close/dispose/finalize on the various platforms. :-( The current TcpClient implementation is that Close/Dispose closes the connection by closing the underlying socket and/or NetworkStream, and finalization doesn't close either. This is the behaviour we use for the here (for , ). (The documentation in MSDN for is still wrong by-the-way, see Microsoft feedback #158480).

Returns the used to send and receive data.
- The underlying NetworkStream. -
GetStream returns a NetworkStream that you can use to send and receive data. The NetworkStream class inherits from the class, which provides a rich collection of methods and properties used to facilitate network communications.
You must call the method, or one of its overloads, first, or the GetStream method will throw an InvalidOperationException. After you have obtained the NetworkStream, call the
method to send data to the remote host. Call the method to receive data arriving from the remote host. Both of these methods block until the specified operation is performed. You can avoid blocking on a read operation by checking the property. A true value means that data has arrived from the remote host and is available for reading. In this case, Read is guaranteed to complete immediately. If the remote host has shutdown its connection, Read will immediately return with zero bytes.
Closing the NetworkStream closes the connection. Similarly Closing, Disposing, or the finalization of the IrDAClient Disposes the NetworkStream. This is new behaviour post 2.0.60828.0.
If you receive a SocketException, use SocketException.ErrorCode to obtain the specific error code. After you have obtained this code, you can refer to the Windows Sockets version 2 API error code documentation in MSDN for a detailed description of the error.
-
The is not connected to a remote host.
The IrDAClient has been closed.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAClient.Dispose(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Releases the unmanaged resources used by the and optionally releases the managed resources.
member.param: true to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources.

members.member:
Closes the and the underlying connection.
-

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDADeviceInfo
member.summary: Provides information about remote devices connected by infrared communications.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDADeviceInfo.#ctor(InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress,System.String,InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints,InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDACharacterSet)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified address, name, hints and character set.
member.param: The IrDA Address of the remote device.
The remote device name.
Hint flags describing the type of device.
Supported character set used for communication.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDADeviceInfo.DeviceAddress
member.summary: Returns the address of the remote device.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDADeviceInfo.DeviceID
member.summary: Provided solely for compatibility with System.Net.IrDA - consider using instead.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDADeviceInfo.DeviceName
member.summary: Gets the name of the device.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDADeviceInfo.CharacterSet
member.summary: Gets the character set used by the server, such as ASCII.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDADeviceInfo.Hints
member.summary: Gets the type of the device, such as a computer.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints
member.summary: Describes an enumeration of possible device types, such as Fax.

member.summary: Unspecified device type.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.PnP
member.summary: A Plug and Play interface.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.PdaAndPalmtop
member.summary: A Pocket PC or similar.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.Computer
member.summary: A personal computer.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.Printer
member.summary: A printer.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.Modem
member.summary: A modem.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.Fax
member.summary: A fax.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.LanAccess
member.summary: A local area network access.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.Extension
member.summary: Contains extended hint bytes.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.Telephony
member.summary: A telephonic device.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.FileServer
member.summary: A personal computer file server.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.IrCOMM
member.summary: Device supports IrCOMM.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAHints.Obex
member.summary: Device supports Object Exchange.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener
member.summary: Places a socket in a listening state to monitor infrared connections from a specified service or network address.
member.remarks: This class monitors a service by specifying a service name or a network address. The listener does not listen until you call one of the methods.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.#ctor(InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The network address to monitor for making a connection.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The name of the service to listen for.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.Server
member.summary: Gets the underlying network .

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.Active
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the is actively listening for client connections.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.LocalEndpoint
member.summary: Gets an representing the local device.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.Start
member.summary: Starts listening for incoming connection requests.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.Start(System.Int32)
member.summary: Starts listening for incoming connection requests with a maximum number of pending connection.
member.param: The maximum length of the pending connections queue.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.Stop
member.summary: Stops the socket from monitoring connections.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.AcceptSocket
member.summary: Creates a new socket for a connection.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.AcceptIrDAClient
member.summary: Creates a client object for a connection when the specified service or endpoint is detected by the listener component.

members.member:
Begins an asynchronous operation to accept an incoming connection attempt.
- An delegate that references the method to invoke when the operation is complete. A user-defined object containing information about the accept operation. This object is passed to the callback delegate when the operation is complete. - An that references the asynchronous creation of the . - The has been closed.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.EndAcceptSocket(System.IAsyncResult)
member.summary: Asynchronously accepts an incoming connection attempt and creates a new to handle remote host communication.
member.param: An returned by a call to the method.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.AcceptSocketAsync(System.Object)
member.summary: Accepts a pending connection request as an asynchronous operation.

members.member:
Begins an asynchronous operation to accept an incoming connection attempt.
- An delegate that references the method to invoke when the operation is complete. A user-defined object containing information about the accept operation. This object is passed to the callback delegate when the operation is complete. - An that represents the asynchronous accept, which could still be pending.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.EndAcceptIrDAClient(System.IAsyncResult)
member.summary: Asynchronously accepts an incoming connection attempt and creates a new to handle remote host communication.
member.param: An returned by a call to the method.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.AcceptIrDAClientAsync(System.Object)
member.summary: Accepts a pending connection request as an asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDAListener.Pending
member.summary: Determines if a connection is pending.
member.returns: true if there is a connection pending; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionLevel
member.summary: Defines additional IrDA socket option levels for the and methods.
member.remarks: Use along with the socket options defined by .

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionLevel.IrLmp
member.summary: The socket option level for use with IrDA sockets along with the options defined in .
member.remarks: Use along with the socket options defined by .

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionName
member.summary: Socket option constants to set IrDA specific connection modes, and get/set IrDA specific features.
member.remarks: Socket option constants to set IrDA specific connection modes, and get/set IrDA specific features: for instance to set IrLMP mode, or get the maximum send size. Pass to /etc and /etc, along with optionLevel IrDASocketOptionLevel.; see the examples below.
member.example:
For instance, where cli is an instance of . In VB.NET, to set IrLMP mode (IrLptMode).
cli.Client.SetSocketOption(IrDASocketOptionLevel.Irlmp, _ IrDASocketOptionName.IrLptMode, _ True)
In C#, to retrieve the maximum send size.
int maxSendSize = (int)cli.Client.GetSocketOption( IrDASocketOptionLevel.Irlmp, IrDASocketOptionName.SendPduLength);

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionName.EnumDevice
member.summary: Gets the list of discovered devices. Is used internally by IrDAClient.DiscoverDevices.
member.remarks: In native terms takes a DEVICE_LIST struct.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionName.IasSet
member.summary: Sets an entry in the local IAS (Information Access Service) database.
member.remarks: In native terms takes a IAS_SET struct.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionName.IasQuery
member.summary: Queries an entry in the peer's IAS (Information Access Service) database.
member.remarks: In native terms takes a IAS_QUERY struct.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionName.SendPduLength
member.summary: Retrieve the maximum send size when using IrLMP directly (). IrLMP requires sent data to fit in one frame.

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionName.ExclusiveMode
member.remarks: Returns an error on all tested platforms. Boolean

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionName.IrLptMode
member.summary: Sets IrLMP mode, disabling TinyTP. Used for instance when printing with IrLPT.
member.remarks: On Windows platforms at least, is ignored on server-side sockets. Boolean

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionName.NineWireMode
member.summary: Sets IrCOMM 9-Wire/Cooked mode. Used for instance when connecting to the modem in a mobile phone (service name IrDA:IrCOMM).
member.remarks: In operation, received IrCOMM control information is discarded and null information is sent. Boolean

member.name: F:InTheHand.Net.Sockets.IrDASocketOptionName.SharpMode
member.summary: Reportedly sets non-IrDA Sharp ASK mode on the Windows CE platform. Presence unverified.

member.name: T:InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint
member.summary: Represents an end point for an infrared connection.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint.#ctor(System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The device identifier.

The Service Name to connect to/listen on eg "OBEX". In the very uncommon case where a connection is to be made to / a server is to listen on a specific LSAP-SEL (port number), then use the form "LSAP-SELn", where n is an integer.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint.#ctor(InTheHand.Net.IrDAAddress,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The device address.

The Service Name to connect to/listen on eg "OBEX". In the very uncommon case where a connection is to be made to / a server is to listen on a specific LSAP-SEL (port number), then use the form "LSAP-SELn", where n is an integer.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint.Address
member.summary: Gets or sets an address for the device.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint.DeviceID
member.summary: Gets or sets an identifier for the device.
member.exception: The specified byte array is null (Nothing in Visual Basic).
The specified byte array is not four bytes long.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint.ServiceName
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the service.

member.name: P:InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint.AddressFamily
member.summary: Gets the address family to which the endpoint belongs.

members.member:
Compares two instances for equality.
- The to compare with the current instance.
- true if is a and equal to the current instance; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns the hash code for this instance.
member.returns: A hash code for the current object.

member.name: M:InTheHand.Net.IrDAEndPoint.ToString
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the IrDAEndPoint.
member.remarks:
The string is in format <DeviceAddress>:<ServiceName>
An example is: "04E20304:OBEX"
member.returns: The string representation of the IrDAEndPoint.
